description:
Architecture for a self-improving GT Arcade that continuously gets better via automated agents.

## Core Concept
A feedback loop where specialized agents (polecats) periodically:
1. Screenshot the game canvas and analyze visual quality
2. Detect GT version changes and adapt the bridge
3. Run QA tests and visual comparisons
4. File findings as beads → auto-dispatch fixes to polecats

## Architecture

### Layer 1: Screenshot Capture
- Add /api/screenshot to bridge (Phaser canvas.toDataURL())
- Add /api/metrics endpoint (fps, draw calls, bead count, agent count)

### Layer 2: Analysis Formulas
- visual-reviewer: Polecat captures game, Claude analyzes for color, layout, sprite quality
- compat-checker: Compare bridge status output vs RIG_MAP, detect unmapped roles/rigs
- qa-agent: Run tsc --noEmit, vite build, visual accessibility checks (WCAG contrast)

### Layer 3: Dispatch
- Each formula outputs actionable beads (1 bead = 1 fixable issue)
- Witness patrol picks up high-priority findings
- Auto-dispatches polecat via gt formula run mol-polecat-work --issue=<id>
- Refinery auto-merges after tests pass

### Layer 4: Verification
- After fix merges, visual-reviewer re-runs and compares before/after
- Closes bead if improved, escalates if regression

## GT Primitives Used
- Formulas (42 available, create 3 new: visual-reviewer, compat-checker, qa-agent)
- Polecats (ephemeral workers for each analysis/fix cycle)
- Dogs (persistent workers for periodic screenshot collection)
- Witness patrol (per-rig oversight, triggers analysis)
- Deacon patrol (cross-rig dispatch, routes findings)
- Beads (track findings, fixes, and verification)

## Implementation Order
1. Add /api/screenshot + /api/metrics to bridge
2. Create visual-reviewer formula
3. Create compat-checker formula
4. Wire into Witness patrol step
5. Create qa-agent formula
6. Add auto-dispatch to Deacon patrol
title: Design: Auto-Sustaining Arcade Improvement Loop

description: Add endpoint to bridge that captures the Phaser game canvas via toDataURL() and returns it as base64 PNG. Also add /api/metrics endpoint returning fps, draw calls, active agents, bead count. These feed the analysis formulas.
title: Bridge: /api/screenshot endpoint (canvas capture)

description: Create mol-visual-reviewer formula. Polecat captures game screenshot via bridge, sends to Claude for analysis. Checks: color consistency with THEME constants, sprite rendering quality, room layout balance, furniture placement, lighting. Files 1 bead per finding with category/severity/location. Runs as convoy-style single-polecat.
title: Formula: visual-reviewer (design quality analysis)

description: Create mol-compat-checker formula. Parses bridge /api/status/parsed, compares rig names against RIG_MAP in ArcadeScene, detects unmapped roles/rigs. Tests agent spawn behavior with new configs. Files missing-rig-mapping beads. Dispatches polecat to add mapping + test.
title: Formula: compat-checker (GT update detection)

description: Create mol-qa-agent formula. Runs tsc --noEmit, vite build, screenshot comparison tests. Checks UI accessibility (WCAG contrast on THEME colors). Files performance/a11y beads. Witness dispatcher routes to appropriate specialist polecat.
title: Formula: qa-agent (automated testing)

description: Extend Witness patrol to trigger visual-reviewer and compat-checker formulas periodically (every N patrol cycles). Extend Deacon patrol to auto-dispatch high-priority design beads to available polecats. Route findings back via mail.
title: Wire auto-dispatch into Witness/Deacon patrol
Bridge server: build proper Express+WS bridge that proxies gt/bd commands, tmux attach, mayor chat, gt status polling, and gt feed streaming to the frontend

close_reason: Multi-room world system implemented: 8 rooms, A* pathfinding, procedural pixel art characters, camera controls, break behavior. Committed fa66172
description:
MAJOR OVERHAUL: Replace the current OfficeScene (colored rectangles on 30x20 grid) with a full Stardew Valley-style multi-room world with procedural pixel art characters.

## Current State
- OfficeScene.ts: 30x20 grid, 32px tiles, rectangles for everything, arrow-key player movement
- No types file, no constants, no pathfinding, no real sprites

## Files to Create

### 1. src/types.ts
All shared interfaces:
```typescript
interface AgentState { id: string; name: string; role: string; rig: string; status: 'working'|'idle'|'walking'|'smoking'|'eating'|'bathroom'|'offline'; position: {x:number,y:number}; targetPosition?: {x:number,y:number}; currentRoom: string; task?: string; }
interface RoomConfig { id: string; name: string; x: number; y: number; width: number; height: number; type: 'department'|'hallway'|'breakroom'|'smoke_area'|'bathroom'|'play_area'; color: number; furniture: FurnitureItem[]; deskPositions: {x:number,y:number}[]; }
interface FurnitureItem { type: 'desk'|'monitor'|'plant'|'toilet'|'arcade_machine'|'vending_machine'|'table'|'chair'|'couch'|'ashtray'; x: number; y: number; width: number; height: number; }
interface AgentVisualTraits { skinTone: number; hairColor: number; hairStyle: number; outfitColor: number; }
interface TileData { walkable: boolean; roomId: string|null; furnitureType: string|null; }
```

### 2. src/constants.ts
```typescript
TILE_SIZE = 16  // pixels (NOT 32 — authentic pixel art scale)
WORLD_WIDTH = 120  // tiles
WORLD_HEIGHT = 80  // tiles
CANVAS_WIDTH = 960
CANVAS_HEIGHT = 640
// Color palettes for rooms, skin tones (6), hair colors (8), outfit colors by rig
SKIN_TONES = [0xFFDBB4, 0xE8B88A, 0xD4956B, 0xB07050, 0x8B5E3C, 0x5C3A1E]
HAIR_COLORS = [0x2C1810, 0x4A3728, 0x8B6F47, 0xD4A76A, 0xC41E3A, 0x1E90FF, 0x7B68EE, 0x2E8B57]
ROOM_COLORS = { planogram: 0x1a3a5c, alc_ai: 0x1a4a3a, arcade: 0x3a1a5c, hallway: 0x2a2a3e, breakroom: 0x3a3a1e, smoke_area: 0x2e2e2e, bathroom: 0x1e3a3a, play_area: 0x3a1a3a }
BREAK_TIMING = { minWorkTime: 30000, maxWorkTime: 120000, breakDuration: 15000, smokeDuration: 20000, bathroomDuration: 10000 }
```

### 3. src/game/world/RoomDefinitions.ts
Export an array of 8 RoomConfig objects arranged in a building layout:
- Top row: planogram dept (0,0 30x25), hallway (30,0 10x25), alc_ai dept (40,0 30x25), arcade dept (70,0 30x25)
- Bottom: break room (0,25 25x20), smoke area (25,25 20x20, partially outdoor), bathroom (45,25 15x20), play area (60,25 30x20)
- Hallway connects top rooms; doorways between floors
- Each room has furniture positions and desk positions for agents

### 4. src/game/world/TileRenderer.ts
A class that draws the world onto a Phaser RenderTexture:
- drawRoom(room: RoomConfig): draws floor tiles (checkerboard pattern with room color), walls (darker shade, 2-tile thick on top), doorways (gaps in walls)
- drawFurniture(item: FurnitureItem): pixel art for each furniture type using Graphics
  - desk: 3x2 tile brown rectangle with lighter top edge
  - monitor: 1x1 tile with blue glow screen
  - plant: 1x1 green blob with pot
  - toilet: 1x1 white rectangle
  - arcade_machine: 1x2 colorful cabinet
  - vending_machine: 1x2 with colored dots
  - table/chair/couch: simple shapes
- All drawing uses this.scene.add.graphics() then draws to a RenderTexture for efficiency

### 5. src/game/world/WorldBuilder.ts
- buildWorld(): creates all rooms, renders to a single large RenderTexture, returns collision grid (boolean[][])
- getCollisionGrid(): TileData[][] — 120x80 grid marking walkable tiles, room IDs, furniture
- getDeskPositions(roomId): returns available desk tile positions
- getDoorways(): returns tile positions of doorways between rooms

### 6. src/game/world/Pathfinding.ts
A* pathfinding on the tile grid:
- findPath(start: {x,y}, end: {x,y}, grid: TileData[][]): {x,y}[]
- Standard A* with manhattan heuristic
- 4-directional movement (no diagonal through walls)
- Returns array of tile coordinates from start to end

### 7. src/game/CameraController.ts
No player character — user is a passive observer:
- Click-and-drag to pan camera
- Mouse wheel to zoom (0.5x to 2x, clamped)
- Number keys 1-8 jump to rooms (smooth lerp transition, ~500ms)
- Smooth camera following when tracking a selected agent
- Camera bounds clamped to world size

### 8. src/game/ArcadeScene.ts (REPLACES OfficeScene.ts)
New main scene that wires everything together:
- create(): WorldBuilder builds world, CameraController attached, creates initial agent sprites
- update(): updates camera, agent movements
- DELETE OfficeScene.ts after creating this

### 9. src/game/sprites/SpriteGenerator.ts
Generates 16x24 pixel art spritesheets per agent:
- generateSpritesheet(name: string, traits: AgentVisualTraits): Phaser.Textures.Texture
- Uses name hash (simple string hash) to deterministically pick traits
- Draws onto a Phaser.GameObjects.Graphics:
  - Frame layout: 4 columns (animation frames) x 6 rows (idle, walk-down, walk-up, walk-left, walk-right, action)
  - Each frame: 16x24 pixels — 8px wide body, 6px head, 2px legs
  - Skin tone for head/hands, hair on top of head (varies by hairStyle), outfit color for body
- Renders to RenderTexture, creates spritesheet texture with frame config

### 10. src/game/sprites/CharacterSprite.ts
Wrapper around Phaser.GameObjects.Container:
- Contains: Phaser.Sprite (from generated spritesheet), name label (BitmapText or Text), status dot (tiny circle: green=working, red=error, gray=idle, yellow=walking)
- select()/deselect() — highlight border when clicked
- updateStatus(status) — changes dot color and current animation
- setDirection(dir) — switches animation to correct walk direction
- Positioned in tile coordinates, rendered at pixel position

### 11. src/game/sprites/AnimationController.ts
- registerAnimations(scene, agentId, textureKey): creates Phaser animations
  - idle: frames 0-3, 4fps, loop
  - walk-down: frames 4-7, 8fps, loop
  - walk-up: frames 8-11, 8fps, loop  
  - walk-left: frames 12-15, 8fps, loop
  - walk-right: frames 16-19, 8fps, loop
  - action: frames 20-23, 4fps, loop (used for sit, eat, smoke)

### 12. src/game/sprites/PortraitGenerator.ts
Generates a 64x96 larger portrait for the info panel:
- generatePortrait(name, traits): returns a base64 PNG data URL
- Draws a larger version of the character face/torso
- Used in the React AgentPanel component

## IMPORTANT NOTES
- Use Phaser 3.80 API (already installed)
- Canvas renderer (already configured)
- pixelArt: true (already configured)
- World is 120x80 tiles at 16px = 1920x1280 pixels (camera shows ~960x640 viewport)
- Update src/components/App.tsx to use ArcadeScene instead of OfficeScene
- Update Phaser config: remove fixed width/height, use scale mode FIT with world bounds
title: Phase 1+2: World System & Procedural Pixel Art Characters

description:
## Conflict Resolution Required

Original MR: gta-wisp-yj65
Branch: polecat/cheedo/gta-7z9@mmfcqd3c (github remote)
Original Issue: gta-7z9
Conflict with target main at: f39fef36d69ec756f5a8633f63ecc553b78f28ec
Branch SHA: 5664486a81e10ded4382f77760d225c4b335f4fd

## Conflicts
- src/components/App.tsx: GiteaActivityPanel import vs SaveLoadPanel import
- src/game/ArcadeScene.ts: separate key handler additions both at same location

Both are ADDITIVE changes (GiteaActivity from HEAD, SaveLoad from branch). Resolution:
1. Keep both imports (GiteaActivityPanel AND SaveLoadPanel/useAutoSave)
2. Keep both state vars (giteaActivityOpen AND saveLoadOpen)
3. Keep both key handlers ('g' for GiteaActivity, F5/Ctrl+S for SaveLoad)
4. Keep both in Escape handler
5. Keep both in JSX rendering

## Instructions
1. Checkout the branch: git checkout -b temp github/polecat/cheedo/gta-7z9@mmfcqd3c
2. Rebase: git rebase main
3. Resolve conflicts (keep both additive sets)
4. Force push: git push -f gitea polecat/cheedo/gta-7z9@mmfcqd3c
5. The MR will be re-processed after resolution.

Note: Branch is on github remote (GitHub account currently suspended). May need to push to gitea remote.
title: Resolve merge conflicts: gta-7z9 save/load system

description:
Create a deacon script that runs after every PR merge to main (or on cron) and auto-updates:

1. README.md — regenerate from template with:
   - Current version from package.json
   - List of features (derived from recent commits/PRs)
   - Status badges
   - Setup instructions
   - Link to latest release

2. CHANGELOG.md — append new entries from git log since last tag:
   - Group by: feat, fix, chore, docs
   - Include PR numbers and authors
   - Format: conventional changelog

3. Version bump — read package.json version, bump patch on every merge:
   - Update package.json version
   - Create git tag
   - Create GitHub release with changelog as body

Script: /home/pratham2/gt/gt_arcade/deacon/scripts/deacon-release.sh
Cron: Run after deacon-pr-review.sh merges something, OR every 2 hours to catch manual merges.

Also do this for ALL active repos:
- freebird-ai/gt-arcade
- Deepwork-AI/ai-planogram
- Deepwork-AI/alc-ai-villa
- Deepwork-AI/gt-mesh

Install cron and register in /home/pratham2/gt/.gt-mesh/cron-registry.yaml

description: The mol-refinery-patrol wisp gta-wisp-0qm2 has test_command='go test ./...' but gt_arcade is a React+Phaser.js project with no Go module. Tests cannot run. Should be updated to match the project stack (e.g. npm test or build check).
title: Refinery test_command misconfigured: go test on JS project

Rethink polecats: not just cleaners. Polecats do real work (long tasks, peer coding). Visualize them as temporary workers who appear, do focused work (typing at borrowed desk, pair programming with crew), then fade when done. Show their assigned bead/task

description: Post-overhaul visual improvements. Quick wins and ambient effects that add life.
title: Design: Visual Polish & Micro-Interactions

description: Clock decoration type in TileRenderer should animate hand positions based on elapsed time. Update drawDecoration() to accept time parameter.
title: Animated clock hands on wall decorations

description: Monitors occasionally skip a frame or pulse alpha 0.95-1.0. Add thin horizontal scanlines. Palette cycle screen content colors (green shift). Arcade machine marquee pulses red/pink.
title: Monitor CRT flicker and scanline effect

description: Window color shifts over 5-minute cycle: daylight #8ab4d8 -> twilight #5a6a8a -> night #2a3a4a. Tied to internal clock. Light rays change color/intensity with cycle.
title: Window day/night cycle

description: New ParticleSystem class. Footstep dust when agents walk (2-3 particles, fade in 200ms). Typing debris near hands (tiny letters float up). Bead cleanup sparkle burst (8-12 gold/cyan particles). Max 50 active particles, viewport culling.
title: Particle system: footstep dust, typing debris, cleanup sparkles

description: Camera zoom 1.0->1.2 over 300ms when selecting agent. Pan-and-hold when polecat finishes cleaning. Brief 100ms time pause on key events (break start, agent online/offline).
title: Camera zoom on agent select + cinematic moments

description: Expand drawFace() to include blink cycle (eyes close/open every 60 frames). Micro-expressions based on activity: happy while playing, focused while typing, relaxed while drinking.
title: Agent idle blink animation and expression range

description: The mol-refinery-patrol molecule has test_command='go test ./...' but gt_arcade is a TypeScript/Node.js project with no go.mod. The test command fails on every merge cycle with 'directory prefix . does not contain main module'. Should be updated to a valid TS test command (e.g. npm test, or left empty if no test suite exists).
title: mol-refinery-patrol test_command misconfigured for gt_arcade rig
Agent click → gt attach: clicking an agent should run 'gt attach <rig>/<role>' and open their tmux session in the terminal panel
Character Upgrade: isometric 3D chibi sprites, 8-dir walking, customization panel, equipment rendering
Mayor chat: connect to mayor's Claude session via bridge server (gt nudge / tmux), show existing conversation history, support real-time chat

description: The mol-refinery-patrol wisp has test_command='go test ./...' but gt_arcade is a TypeScript/Vite project with no Go module. Running 'go test ./...' always fails with 'directory prefix . does not contain main module'. Should be updated to a valid TS check (e.g., 'npx tsc --noEmit') or left empty.
title: Fix test_command config: repo is TypeScript, not Go

description:
Rig identity bead for gt_arcade.

prefix: gta
state: active
title: gt_arcade
Character customization UI: working panel to change hat, hair, face, outfit for each agent. Persist choices

description: Complete UI/UX rework based on user testing. See notes for full details.
notes: All items implemented. See commit for details.
title: GT Arcade v2: Major UI/UX Redesign from User Feedback

description: Redesign BeadsPanel with proper table formatting. Group by priority (P0 red header, P1 orange, P2 yellow). Each row: ID | Title | Status chip (colored) | Rig | Assignee | Est. Clickable rows expand to show description + acceptance criteria. Add filters: by rig, by status (open/blocked/done), by assignee. Add search. Sortable columns. The current panel has these features but the table rendering and scrolling is poor — needs pixel-perfect alignment, smooth scrolling, fixed header. Make it feel like a game inventory screen.
title: BeadsPanel Redesign: game-style inventory table with priority groups
GitHub account suspended - git push/fetch to origin blocked
Screenshot gallery mode: press F12 to capture pixel-perfect game screenshot with GBA frame overlay, save to downloads, share-ready dimensions
gt feed integration: show activity feed (mail, bead updates, agent events) as a scrolling ticker or panel in the dashboard
Bead viewer panel: show all beads/tasks from bd list, bd ready, bd blocked in a game-style UI overlay. Watch beads in real-time
